rows.requirement: Submitting a query calls the /query API
rows.planned_check: QueryView.test.tsx test 'submits query and renders answer + citations' asserts global fetch mock called once with URL '/query' and a JSON body containing the typed query text after form submit.
Same test: findByTestId('query-answer') contains the mocked response's answer string.

Same test: findByTestId('query-citations') present; getByRole('link', { name: <citation path> }) resolves for each mocked citation, confirming both visibility (text content) and clickability (anchor/Link role).

rows.requirement: Test spec: component test mocking the /query response, asserting answer text and citation list render correctly
rows.planned_check: QueryView.test.tsx exists, uses vi.fn()/vi.stubGlobal to mock fetch (the /query response), and its assertions cover both answer text and citation list rendering -- satisfied by the two checks above.

rows.requirement: Error path is handled gracefully (not in acceptance criteria explicitly but required for a usable submit flow / non-200 statuses documented in api/routes/query.go)
rows.planned_check: QueryView.test.tsx test 'renders an error message when the /query call fails' asserts data-testid='query-error' renders the failure message instead of throwing/crashing.

rows.requirement: Empty query is not submitted (basic input validation, mirrors api/routes/query.go's 400 on empty query)
rows.planned_check: QueryView.test.tsx test 'does not submit an empty query' asserts fetch is not called when the form is submitted with blank/whitespace-only input.

rows.requirement: No regression to already-verified 6.1.1 router test
rows.planned_check: App.test.tsx's /query row updated to assert data-testid='query-form' (always-rendered) instead of the removed on-mount 'query-status'; full App.test.tsx suite re-run in self-consistency.
self-consistency.json records `npm run build` output/exit code inside ui/.
